title: Behaviour in systemd mode WRT cgroups is different than documented
body:
### Issue Description

The `podman-run(1)` man page of my podman-4.7.0-1.fc38.x86_64 installation, as well as https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/source/markdown/options/systemd.md say:

    Running the container in systemd mode causes the following changes:
 
    Podman mounts tmpfs file systems on the following directories
        /run
        /run/lock
        /tmp
        /sys/fs/cgroup/systemd
        /var/lib/journal
    [...]

However, checking the mount points in my rootless container, I don't see that advertized `/sys/fs/cgroup/systemd`. Instead, mount options on `/sys/fs/cgroup` seem to be different.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Read `man podman-run(1)` about the impact on the `/sys/fs/cgroup` in the systemd mode.
2. `$ podman run --systemd=false --rm registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora mount | grep cgroup`
3. `$ podman run --systemd=always --rm registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora mount | grep cgroup`


### Describe the results you received

```
$ podman run --systemd=false --rm registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora mount | grep cgroup
cgroup2 on /sys/fs/cgroup type cgroup2 (ro,nosuid,nodev,noexec,relatime,seclabel,nsdelegate,memory_recursiveprot)
```

```
$ podman run --systemd=always --rm registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora mount | grep cgroup
cgroup2 on /sys/fs/cgroup type cgroup2 (rw,relatime,seclabel,nsdelegate,memory_recursiveprot)
```

### Describe the results you expected

I would expect the results to match the documentation, or rather, have the documentation match the results. The mention of `/sys/fs/cgroup/systemd` should be removed, and an explanation about `/sys/fs/cgroup` being read-write instead of read-only added.

### podman info output

### Additional information

comments.body:
`/sys/fs/cgroup/systemd` is created only on cgroup v1.

on cgroup v2 there is no separate named hierarchy used by cgroup, there is only one mount point for cgroups.

Opened a PR to clarify it: https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/20529
